code_review.project_type: web application — AI-assisted software project planning tool; locally-run Dash app also published as a standalone PyPI package
code_review.architecture: layered (presentation / business logic / session & persistence) with a sequential pipeline pattern across four AI agents (Reviewer → Brainstormer → StackAdvisor → Phaser); loose coupling between pipeline stages allows users to jump in at any stage by uploading prior output; client-side credential storage via Dash dcc.Store (browser localStorage)
languages: JavaScript (embedded inline strings in Dash clientside callbacks only)

dependencies.name: dash
dependencies.purpose: core web framework (Flask-based, reactive UI via callbacks)

dependencies.name: dash-mantine-components
dependencies.purpose: React/Mantine-based UI component library for Dash

dependencies.name: litellm
dependencies.purpose: unified multi-provider LLM abstraction (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Cohere, Mistral)

dependencies.name: mcp
dependencies.purpose: Model Context Protocol — integrates Tavily web search tool into agents

notes: test coverage exists for 6 modules (agents, app_constants, project_manager, providers, session, tavily_mcp) but presentation and streaming layers (app.py, layouts.py, callbacks.py, streaming.py) have no direct test coverage
single test_agents.py covers all four agent modules — coverage may be partial
no CI/CD pipeline detected (.github/workflows or equivalent); all quality gates are manual via make targets despite CONTRIBUTING.md and CLA.md suggesting external contributions are anticipated
py.typed marker present and mypy strict mode enabled — package declares itself fully typed
all four agents can invoke outbound HTTP requests via Tavily/MCP web search — relevant for latency, error handling, and future sandboxing
planning artifacts (code_review.json, vision.json, etc.) are written to a .spec4/ folder on the local filesystem — relevant for multi-user or containerised deployments
leftover [tool.hatch.build.targets.sdist] config in pyproject.toml has no effect with uv_build backend — minor inconsistency
from __future__ import annotations used across agent modules for deferred annotation evaluation
.vscode/launch.json present — project is actively developed in VS Code with a configured debug launcher
E501 (line-too-long) suppressed per-file for agents/*.py (long system prompt strings) and app.py (inline JavaScript strings)
